title: Luffy and Sanji Leave with Nami
description: On pages 5–7, luffy and sanji leave with nami as wapol comes back to reclaim his kingdom as Luffy, Sanji, and Nami prepare for the dangerous climb toward Dr. Kureha.
scene: Luffy and Sanji Leave with Nami. Wapol comes back to reclaim his kingdom as Luffy, Sanji, and Nami prepare for the dangerous climb toward Dr. Kureha. The focus is the story beat, character decisions, and dialogue driving this part.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: It's getting pretty cold with all this wind...

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: Why the hell are you wearing sandals in this weather? Just looking at'em is making me feel cold!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: In any case, did ya know that people in snowy countries don't sleep?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: because they'll die if they do.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: That's the dumbest thing I've ever heard.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: It's true. That's what a guy once told me.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: nope, from a guy in a bar.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Then why was there a bed in that Dalton dude's home?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: You're right! Then that must be when it's time for him to die...

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Well, let me ask you this.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Did you know that the women of snowy countries all have beautiful smooth skin?

dialogue.speaker: Sanji
dialogue.text: You know how when it's cold, you? Rub your skin together?

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Having smooth white skin is the trademark of women in snowy countries.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: That's because the colour of the snow seeps inside their skin.

dialogue.speaker: Wapol
dialogue.text: You're the last person I want to hear that from!

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: Would you freakin' knock it off already!

summary: This part centers on luffy and sanji leave with nami, clarifying the immediate conflict and how it moves the chapter toward its next major turn.
